title: abrus schimperi subsp. africanus
intro: Abrus schimperi subsp. africanus, also known as the 'small-leaf jigger', is a plant species belonging to the Fabaceae family. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in West African traditional medicine, where it is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including wound healing, fever reduction, and treatment of skin conditions such as eczema and acne. In herbal preparations, the leaves and roots of the plant are often dried and used to make infusions or decoctions for their medicinal benefits.
